text.title: Spec Example: Ecommerce Cart Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Example: Ecommerce Cart. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Example is fully filled in — no placeholders, no placeholders anywhere in the example body.
Authors who adapt the example record archetype = 'ecommerce-cart' in front-matter.
Example version matches spec-structure version; updates ship in the same PR.
Every section in the adapted spec is either rewritten OR explicitly marked 'kept from example'.
rule.rationale: Verbatim copies signal lack of engagement.

rule.statement: Example covers a single-team well-bounded feature; multi-team scope is excluded.
rule.rationale: Wider scope belongs in spec-advanced-guidelines example.

rule.statement: Example AC are all testable binary checks; vague AC removed.
rule.rationale: Setting the bar in the example forces the bar in adapted specs.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-example-ecommerce-cart.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
